name: meeting-importance
goal: Reduce meeting processing overhead from 7-9 meetings/day to <5 minutes total attention by auto-inferring importance and processing light meetings with minimal extraction

userStories.title: Extend CalendarEvent with organizer and recurrence data
userStories.description: Add organizer and recurringEventId fields to CalendarEvent type. Update Google Calendar provider to map these from API response.
acceptanceCriteria: CalendarEvent has optional organizer?: { name: string; email?: string; self?: boolean }
CalendarEvent has optional recurringEventId?: string
GoogleEvent type includes organizer from Google Calendar API schema
mapGoogleEvent() maps organizer.email, organizer.displayName, organizer.self
mapGoogleEvent() maps recurringEventId if present
Existing tests pass (npm test -- packages/core/test/integrations/calendar)
Use optional chaining for organizer fields (Pre-Mortem R2)

userStories.title: Add importance field and inference logic
userStories.description: Add importance to MeetingForSave. Create inferMeetingImportance() function with rules based on organizer, attendee count, and agenda linking.
acceptanceCriteria: MeetingForSave has optional importance?: 'skip' | 'light' | 'normal' | 'important'
MeetingForSave has optional recurring_series_id?: string
New function inferMeetingImportance(event: CalendarEvent, options?: { hasAgenda?: boolean }): 'light' | 'normal' | 'important'
Inference: organizer.self === true → 'important'
Inference: attendees.length === 2 → 'important'
Inference: attendees.length <= 3 → 'normal'
Inference: attendees.length >= 5 && !organizer.self → 'light'
Inference: hasAgenda === true → at least 'normal'
saveMeetingFile() writes importance to frontmatter when provided
Tests for inferMeetingImportance() covering all rules
All new fields are optional for backward compatibility (Pre-Mortem R1)

userStories.title: Create light extraction prompt and mode
userStories.description: Add buildLightExtractionPrompt() for minimal extraction. Add mode parameter to extractMeetingIntelligence() with 'light', 'normal', and 'thorough' options.
acceptanceCriteria: New ExtractionMode = 'light' | 'normal' | 'thorough' type
extractMeetingIntelligence() accepts optional mode?: ExtractionMode (default: 'normal')
buildLightExtractionPrompt() extracts summary only
buildLightExtractionPrompt() extracts up to 2 learnings focused on domain insights, strategic decisions, or user feedback
buildLightExtractionPrompt() explicitly instructs: Do NOT extract action items or operational decisions
buildLightExtractionPrompt() is ~50% shorter than normal prompt
LIGHT_LIMITS = { actionItems: 0, decisions: 0, learnings: 2 }
THOROUGH_LIMITS = { actionItems: 10, decisions: 7, learnings: 7 } with no confidence floor
mode === 'light' uses buildLightExtractionPrompt() and LIGHT_LIMITS
mode === 'thorough' uses normal prompt with THOROUGH_LIMITS
Tests for light mode extraction (mock LLM, verify shape)
Keep existing CATEGORY_LIMITS as NORMAL_LIMITS (Pre-Mortem R7)

userStories.title: Speaking ratio analysis and importance upgrade
userStories.description: Create calculateSpeakingRatio() to analyze transcript speaker labels. At processing time, upgrade light → normal if user spoke 40%+ of the meeting.
acceptanceCriteria: New function calculateSpeakingRatio(transcript: string, ownerName: string): number | undefined
Parses speaker labels in format **Name | HH:MM** or **Name | MM:SS**
Counts words per speaker (not just line count)
Returns ratio of owner's words to total words (0-1)
Returns undefined if no speaker labels found (graceful degradation)
Owner name matching is case-insensitive and handles partial matches
Tests for ratio calculation with various transcript formats
Tests for missing speaker labels returning undefined
Never crash on malformed transcripts (Pre-Mortem R4)

userStories.title: Auto-approve light meetings and add skipped status
userStories.description: Update processMeetingExtraction() to check importance. Light meetings auto-approve all items. Add 'skipped' as valid status. Reprocessing uses thorough mode.
acceptanceCriteria: ItemStatus type includes 'skipped' (in addition to 'approved' | 'pending')
processMeetingExtraction() accepts optional importance?: Importance parameter
importance === 'light': All items have status: 'approved' (auto-approve)
importance === 'skip': Return immediately with empty result, set status: 'skipped'
CLI meeting extract reads importance from meeting frontmatter
--importance <level> flag overrides frontmatter
If file has status: processed or status: approved, use mode 'thorough' (reprocessing)
Integrate speaking ratio: if importance === 'light' and calculateSpeakingRatio() > 0.4, upgrade to 'normal'
Tests for auto-approve logic
Tests for reprocessing detection
Atomic write — all items + status in single file write (Pre-Mortem R6)

userStories.title: Wire importance inference into calendar pull
userStories.description: When pulling calendar events, infer importance for each meeting and include in saved meeting file.
acceptanceCriteria: pullCalendar() calls inferMeetingImportance() for each event
Importance is passed to meeting save/sync operation
Meeting frontmatter includes importance: <level> field after sync
If agenda is linked, hasAgenda: true is passed to inference
Integration test: pull calendar event → verify importance in frontmatter
Importance persists in frontmatter for later extraction (Pre-Mortem R8)

userStories.title: Update documentation and LEARNINGS.md
userStories.description: Document the new importance system in relevant files.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/integrations/LEARNINGS.md updated with importance inference pattern
packages/core/src/services/LEARNINGS.md updated with extraction modes
.pi/expertise/core/PROFILE.md Component Map updated with speaking ratio function
CLI command documentation updated for --importance flag
